findings.desc: Multiple unguarded STORAGE writes (potential storage corruption)
Arithmetic without bounds check (potential overflow)
STORAGE write (SSTORE) — state mutation
Modulo arithmetic (division-by-zero risk if divisor unguarded)

oracle_finding: Unchecked modulo operation can cause SSTORE index underflow
oracle_reasoning: The line `uint256_t t25 = t12 - t24;` performs an unchecked modulo operation (`t22 % t23`) which can result in an underflow. This underflow is then used to calculate the SSTORE index `t25` without any guard.

oracle_finding: Unchecked arithmetic overflow in SSTORE index calculation
oracle_reasoning: The SSTORE index calculation `t26 = t26 % t15` can overflow due to unchecked modulo operation. There is no guard to prevent this potential overflow.

oracle_finding: Unchecked arithmetic (modulo) leads to unindexed SSTORE
oracle_reasoning: The modulo operation `t12 % t21` results in the index for `STORAGE[t22] = t9;`. This index is not guarded by any conditionals.

oracle_finding: Unchecked modulo operation can lead to out-of-bounds SSTORE access
oracle_reasoning: The code performs `t23 = t12 % t22`, where `t12` is the result of a comparison and `t22` is loaded from memory. This modulo operation can result in an out-of-bounds index for the SSTORE at `STORAGE[0xda8be9b]` if `t12` is greater than or equal to `t22`.
